counters.description: her short range trades are very strong with her lethality build, and you have no mobility to easily escape her ultimate, making it high guaranteed damage onto you whether she combos it with her support's cc or not. ideally you'll want an engage support to lock her down and seek out trades more extended than she wants.

twitch shines against immobile adcs who make easy pray for his stealth engages, and on top of being immobile, aphelios also struggles with being outranged, which makes him particularly weak against spray and pray engages. his pre-6 laning is his weakest stage and this is where you have to try and snowball if the matchup allows. if you get far enough ahead, you may get too strong for him to bully you later.

lethal tempo/on-hit varus: out-dpses you at all stages, slightly out-ranges you except when you have calibrum, but your immediate burst at least is higher. cleanse makes this matchup a lot easier to survive, exhaust makes him a lot easier to duel early on.

comet/lethality varus: 80% of his damage will come from spamming his abilities way outside of your range, leaving you no room for retaliation. can be manageable if you have a hook support, otherwise he'll be impossible to ignore but impossible to address.

will out-damage you 90% of the time that you're in his range, leaving you with not much recourse except ignoring him. thankfully, it's usually not too hard to do this as his range is very limited, so you should be able to survive just fine so long as you try and avoid interacting with him. you may also be able to kill him if your support is able to cc lock him. can be extremely threatening if he has a hook support and your support is also weak to engage. regardless, always run exhaust against him to shut down his damage so you can survive being within his range when necessary.

samira herself is not innately that problematic for aphelios, but the bigger problem is that she's almost always paired with an engage support who will be a problem for him, and she plays to their win condition well. run exhaust to shut down her burst when she finds engages. she's not scary if she can't dash in on you.

she beats you in extended fights, her slows make it easy for her to force extended fights, and you typically don't have the burst to avoid fights getting to the point of becoming extended. cleanse is completely mandatory against her.

ziggs plays entirely through his spammable q with far too much range for you to fight back, on top of having insane zone control from his e and insane disengage in his w. there is little chance of winning this lane if you don't have a hook support such as blitzcrank.

even your ability damage is mostly basic attacks, and nilah w dodges basic attacks. she also has an easy time diving onto you where she out-dpses you and outbursts you. you'll want to run ghost and rush berserker's greaves to most effectively kite her, and disengage will be the best tool your support can provide you in this matchup.

the extreme range of her abilities makes it incredibly hard to interact with her, while she has an easy time poking and comboing you. your reward for making it through this lane is that she arguably scales just as well as you do, giving you no light at the end of the tunnel. engage supports can make this lane manageable, but she's definitely a natural counter to aphelios.
you are your own greatest enemy. never forget that.

synergies.description: 3 out of 5 aphelios abilities interact with braum's passive to stack it faster, and onslaught stacks it pretty much instantly, making their synergy together crazy good. even aphelios's ultimate stacks braum's passive. he is likely the best support you could have for facing engage supports, but deals quite poorly with ranged matchups as his engage is quite lacking.

aphelios is quite short ranged and his greatest weakness is being outranged. blitzcrank is the perfect counter to this weakness by bringing enemies into aphelios, effectively neutralizing the threat of matchups that should be unwinnable for aphelios. often blitzcrank will be the single best possible pick for a lane with no other support coming close.

thresh synergizes quite well with immobile adcs as his lantern provides defensive (or offensive) mobility where otherwise none would exist, making up for a huge weakness of theirs. aphelios is no exception in this regard.

rakan is one of the best peeling supports while also having some great short-range engage post-6. his laning isn't all that good but his peeling is good enough to easily help aphelios survive, and his engage range can be enough to function against mid-range matchups, while he scales amazingly into the mid-late game.

janna specializes in peeling, and her ultimate is possibly the best disengage in the game. she works best against melee divers/engagers but doesn't provide much in the way of engage to deal with higher range matchups. she has decent poking capabilities, but doesn't trade all that well compared to other enchanters.

sona shines into all the same short-ranged adcs that you do, and her ultimate sets up a free ultimate for you as well as providing decent peel. she is also the only support whose scaling matches yuumi's. she can be quite effective in matchups where you both have an easy time scaling up.

renata's hooky ability is a fantastic disengage,  her ultimate is an amazing counterengage to punish engages, and her revive ability is an amazing tool for helping you outplay and survive being engaged on. she is definitely up there as one of the best anti-dive peeling supports in the game, geared more towards helping you beat those lanes rather than around trying to survive them. that being said, she also scales tremendously well.

it's hard for mage supports to really synergize badly with anyone since they have such strong tools for just winning a lane by themselves. vel'koz definitely fits into that camp with his extremely long range poke combined with extremely high damage from his ultimate in fights, meaning that although he's not much good at keeping you safe, he also greatly punishes enemies for focusing you and ignoring him, and does a great job of bullying the lane by himself. he's also too long range to be engaged on by most engages that aren't long range hooks.

with a kit so heavily aggression-focused this is not the ideal hook support to pair with aphelios, who also perhaps lacks the sort of explosive damage that pyke would like to see in his adc to help the two snowball.

milio's strengths lie in shielding, sustain, cc removal, and most of all in amplifying your basic attacks with a range increase. unfortunately this doesn't synergize as amazingly with aphelios as you might think, as he's quite ability-focused in the early-game. he doesn't get much out of the range increase compared to actual attack-based adcs until much later on.

maokai offers a great mix of peel and engage. despite not being a hook support, his ultimate can engage from deceptively long ranges, and his combo of rooting an enemy and pushing them into his adc can very easily catch out the highest range champions, and serves as a fairly good engage even without his ultimate into low range matchups. there is not much that maokai won't work against, though he's not necessarily the best possible pick into every lane.

alistar is one of the worst engage supports to pick when you really need engage due to how short-ranged his engage is. however, he excels in being engaged upon since he can turn it around on his attacker by knocking them up and knocking their adc away. alternatively if aphelios is the one being engaged on, he can counterengage onto the adc who either *has* to tank the alistar combo, or has to not follow up at all, also advantaging the aphelios lane. alternatively, alistar could also knock up the engager and knock them into the turret, also greatly disadvantaging them in the fight.

as far as mage supports go, neeko is one of the best at functioning as a hard engage, particularly with the ability to disguise herself as a minion/control ward. more importantly, she's also very good at punishing divers by popping them with her ultimate whenever they get close, making it hard for them to engage onto her or aphelios. she works most effectively into melee divers and lower ranged champs in general.

nami provides a lot of peel and sustain, making her strong against melee supports without heavy dive/engage, and decent into poke-based lanes. her ultimate into bubble combo can even provide decent engage into such matchups.

lux's greatest synergy with aphelios is her root can set up a free ultimate for you, but that's about it. she doesn't have any real engage and her q lockdown isn't that long, so she doesn't have many tools to enable you. she at least has some peel for you, but not much.
senna has poor peel, no engage and subpar sustain. basically no tools to deal with aphelios's weaknesses in lane.

her peeling is decent, but is still quite weak in lane to engage/dive supports and offers very little help against mage supports too. additionally pix damage is higher when you have more attack speed and you spend most of the game with low attack speed, making their synergy in that regard suboptimal too. she can work when laning against other enchanters though, but would never be optimal.

his hook pulls the target to him a lot less than it pulls himself into the target, making him a lot worse at helping you close the gap onto higher range lanes than other hook supports do. he'll still be useful in lanes where you need the engage, but a lot less than other choices.

morgana has very little synergy with aphelios besides her root potentially setting up a free ultimate for you, which makes her only good when used as a counterpick to the enemy support. she works most effectively against engage supports, especially those whose strongest cc isn't hard to react to, so better against leona than nautilus for example.

as far as mage supports go, zyra probably has the single most effective engage and lockdown of all of them, and also decent peel. isn't as effective against divers as neeko, but does punish low range laners a bit more.

karma's good at obtaining lane priority through a mix of poke and strong pushing power, while also behaving similarly to an enchanter with shields and movement speed boosts. her synergy with aphelios is a bit lacking, but she can work into other enchanters for a strong lane phase.

leona is incredibly tanky for a support, which makes her excell against hook supports in particular since she can just tank the hook and turn the fight around. for aphelios, she's very good when used for this purpose, but if you want engage, there are much better engage supports to go for, since her e range is quite limited, her ultimate is unreliable as well as easily countered with cleanse.

his synergy with aphelios is virtually nonexistant, but he does actually do okay into some of the diver supports that aphelios hates. pre-6 he can poke them down very effectively, and post-6 them engaging onto brand can lead to a 1-for-1 trade as a result of dying to brand's ult rather than the clean pick they should hope for onto a squishy mage. he's just not that great into enchanters or generally higher ranged picks that have no need to get close to him.

too likely that he will ult at the same time as you and deny your ultimate. can be ok into enchanters if premade and properly coordinated, as his ultimate can instead start being used to set up your ultimates instead of denying them. his ultimate can also set up some nice engages onto certain mages, but like aphelios he's also weak into hook supports.

doesn't have amazing peel, but does have great range and a pretty good lane phase, provided she's not facing engage. she performs decently into the shorter range mages, and can effectively bully melee supports with poor engage. her e has decent synergy with gravitum and her ultimate can set up a nice ult for you as well.

yuumi synergizes best with mobile adcs, since her adcs mobility is also her mobility, and if her adc is vulnerable then she is vulnerable. this makes aphelios a poor fit for her since he is a rather vulnerable adc, but it can work against low-pressure enchanters in lane who can't easily punish aphelios for having a low-presence support.
compared to other mage supports, xerath shines playing way further back. this leaves aphelios alone on the frontlines, or hanging back and letting xerath 1v2 the lane. it's not ideal either way.

even for a mage support, swain plays more like the second carry of the lane rather than as a support. aphelios is an adc who definitely needs support to get by. that being said, swain works quite well into engage/hook supports since he can tank hooks and turn fights around, so he wouldn't be too bad into those lanes for aphelios, but there are definitely better options.
ashe's slows make her great with skillshot-based adcs, but aphelios's only consistent skillshot is on a 120 second cooldown. anything else ashe offers, some other support would do better.

his 'engages' are diving too far forwards with too little lockdown for you to provide much in response. if you need an engage support, there are far better ones for aphelios. green probably would work best with him so you can land your burstiest ultimate for free every time he goes in, and panth would do most of the other damage needed.

zilean works best with adcs that want to go in. aphelios only really has one weapon he wants to go in with, and even then he probably still wouldn't want to do it with zilean. however, he can be effective vs dive supports who have to expend a lot of effort just to kill you the first time, before you just revive.

taric is dramatically more effective into melee supports than he is into ranged ones. his passive gives him a lot of dps and allows him to constantly reset his healing, provided only that he has someone he can basic attack on repeat from melee range. champions that can kite him are very bad for this, but he is brutally effective against melee supports.

3 out of 5 aphelios abilities interact with braum's passive to stack it faster, and onslaught stacks it pretty much instantly, making their synergy together crazy good. even aphelios's ultimate stacks braum's passive.
